explanation: This LTL property expresses that whenever the system is in either PCVMode or PSVMode and the expiratoryPauseButton is pressed by the operator, then for at most 60 seconds it must be true that expiratoryPause is active, and both inValveClose (inspiratory valve closed) and outValveClose (expiratory valve closed) are true. However, this condition can be interrupted if the operator releases the button (not captured in atomic propositions, so assumed the signal resets on release). The template chosen encodes that as soon as the button is pressed under the correct modes, the desired state is enforced for at most the duration (here, 60 seconds = N_DURATION).

The requirement states that in PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) and PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) modes, the system must allow the operator to press and hold a single button to trigger an Expiratory Pause—this action closes both the inspiratory and expiratory valves at the end of the expiration phase, for as long as the button is held, but no longer than 60 seconds. Among the provided atomic propositions, 'expiratoryPauseButton' models the operator pressing the button, 'expiratoryPause' models the system being in the Expiratory Pause, 'inValveClose' and 'outValveClose' indicate the states of their respective valves, and 'expiratoryPhase' can be used to pin the behavior to the end of expiration.

The most likely LTL property focuses on the assertion that whenever in PCV or PSV mode, if the operator sets the expiratoryPauseButton at the relevant time, then as long as this button is held, the system must keep both valves closed and remain in Expiratory Pause for at most 60 seconds.

Due to the lack of an atomic proposition for button release, it is most abstractly captured by asserting that whenever the button is pressed, the system must transition into Expiratory Pause, with both valves closed, for a maximum of 60 seconds. This is directly represented by F[0, 60] (eventually within 60 seconds), conditioned on the button press event. The template 'G (bool_exp1 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))' captures this: whenever (bool_exp1) occurs (expiratoryPauseButton & (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPhaseEnd), then within 60 seconds, (bool_exp3): (expiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose) must hold.

Thus: bool_exp1 = (expiratoryPauseButton & (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPhaseEnd); bool_exp3 = (expiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose); N_DURATION = 60.

This LTL property expresses that in either PCVMode or PSVMode, whenever the operator (user_equalTo_operator) presses the expiratoryPauseButton during the expiratoryPhase, both the inspiratory and expiratory valves (inValveClose and outValveClose) must remain closed (expiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose) for the duration for which the button is held, but not exceeding 60 seconds. When the button is released or 60 seconds elapse, the pause ends. This directly reflects the requirement that in PCV and PSV modes, the operator can press a button to initiate an expiratory pause at the end of the expiration phase, closing both valves as long as the button is held but no more than 60 seconds. N_DURATION=60 expresses the temporal bound on the expiratory pause.

The best-fitting template is one expressing that, whenever in PCV or PSV mode and the operator is requesting an expiratory pause (expiratoryPauseButton), then the system must maintain the Expiratory Pause state, both inValveClose and outValveClose (i.e., both valves closed), for as long as the button is held but not longer than 60 seconds. This can be represented by: in PCVMode or PSVMode, when the operator presses expiratoryPauseButton, the system should (for up to 60 seconds or until button release) ensure Expiratory Pause state (expiratoryPause, inValveClose, outValveClose). Because the atomic proposition 'expiratoryPause' exists, and inValveClose and outValveClose can be inferred from the description, for LTL, we capture: Whenever (PCVMode or PSVMode) AND expiratoryPauseButton, then for up to 60 time units, 'expiratoryPause AND inValveClose AND outValveClose' holds. Thus, template 14 is used: G (cond -> (F[0,N_DURATION] action)); where cond is '(PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton' and action is 'expiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose'. The requirement states that in PCV and PSV modes, there must be a possibility to press and hold a button (expiratoryPauseButton) to initiate an Expiratory Pause. During this pause, both the inspiratory and expiratory valves should remain closed (inValveClose and outValveClose), and this state should persist only while the button is pressed and for no longer than 60 seconds. The best LTL encoding for the core of this requirement is to require that when the expiratoryPauseButton is pressed, both valves are closed for up to 60 seconds. This is achieved by using template 12: 'G (bool_exp2 -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (G bool_exp3)))', with bool_exp2 as (expiratoryPauseButton), and bool_exp3 as (inValveClose & outValveClose). This captures that as long as the button is held, for a maximum of 60 seconds, both valves must remain closed, corresponding to the described function.

The requirement is about enabling an Expiratory Pause via a single button press in PCV or PSV mode that closes both inspiratory and expiratory valves at the end of expiration, lasting only while the button is held, and at most 60 seconds. In LTL, this can be described as a response: whenever (PCVMode or PSVMode) and the expiratoryPauseButton are pressed at the end of the expiratory phase, the valves (inValveClose & outValveClose) must be active within the next 60 seconds. explanation: The requirement states that in PCV and PSV mode, pressing and holding a button initiates an Expiratory Pause, closing both the inspiratory and expiratory valves at the end of expiration, but never for longer than 60 seconds. The property G((PCVMode | PSVMode) -> ((!expiratoryPauseButton | !expiratoryPhase) U ((expiratoryPauseButton & expiratoryPhase) & (inValveClose & outValveClose & X ((!expiratoryPauseButton | !expiratoryPhase) | F[0,60] (!(expiratoryPauseButton & expiratoryPhase))))))) expresses that in PCV or PSV mode, whenever the Expiratory Pause button is held at the end of expiration (expiratoryPhase), both valves are closed (inValveClose & outValveClose). This condition holds as long as the button is held and the phase is correct ONLY up to 60 seconds, after which the pause must be released. U ensures the behavior only during the required interval. N_DURATION is set to 60 as per limit.

This LTL property ensures that whenever the system is in either PCV Mode or PSV Mode and the operator presses the expiratoryPauseButton at the end of the expiration phase (indicated by expiratoryPhaseEnd), then the system closes both the inspiratory valve (inValveClose) and the expiratory valve (outValveClose). This closed state must be maintained as long as the operator holds the button, but no longer than 60 seconds. The template enforces that, upon the proper combination of conditions (mode, button, phase), the system must guarantee the expected safety behavior.

This property states that whenever the system is in either PCVMode or PSVMode, pressing the 'expiratoryPauseButton' results in an 'expiratoryPause' (the pause is active, which is likely controlled by the system). Furthermore, during the pause, both the inspiratory and expiratory valves are commanded to close ('inValveClose' and 'outValveClose'). This will remain true for up to 60 seconds (N_DURATION=60), aligning with the maximum allowed pause hold from the original requirement. The property thus guarantees that, under the appropriate mode, pressing the button initiates the correct Expiratory Pause feature with the specified valve state, and for the specified maximal duration. Operator holding and interaction detail (button-hold logic) is implicitly captured by requiring the condition to hold as long as the signal is present, and the upper bound matches the time constraint.
